# Figure description: Annual Report 2020.png

**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type
Satellite imagery base map overlaid with mineral tenure (tenement) boundaries. This is an index/tenure map showing the spatial distribution of mining and exploration licences around Coe, Western Australia, displayed over a true-colour or false-colour satellite image.

---

## Coverage and Subject
The map covers an area approximately centred on **Coe**, a well-known gold mining town in the Murchison region of Western Australia. The spatial extent spans roughly **580,000E to 592,000E** and **6,959,000N to 6,977,000N** (GDA94/MGA Zone 50 or similar UTM projection). The map covers approximately **12 km (E–W) × 18 km (N–S)**, with a scale bar indicating 0–2 km increments.

Key geographic features visible include:
- The town of **Coe** (labelled, eastern-central portion)
- **Great Cuchulainn Pit** (white star symbol, south-central)
- **South Cuchulainn** (white star symbol)
- **Veldt** (white star symbol, near South Cuchulainn)
- Blue lines representing **drainage/watercourses** crossing the area
- Roads/tracks shown as **black lines**

---

## Key Content

**Tenement boundaries** are shown as white (active/current) and black dashed (other tenure or historical) polygons. The tenements are a mix of:

- **P (Prospecting Licences):** The majority of tenements shown in white outlines
- **M (Mining Licences):** Smaller, typically irregular polygons
- **E (Exploration Licence):** One large block in the northeast (E 2000871)

The white-outlined tenements form a roughly **north–south trending corridor** of prospecting licences west and southwest of Coe, broadly following what appears to be a greenstone belt or structural corridor visible in the satellite imagery as darker, more rugged terrain contrasting with the surrounding reddish-brown regolith.

The black dashed tenement outlines suggest broader, overlapping or historical tenure coverage across the wider area.

Three historical/significant mine sites (Great Cuchulainn Pit, South Cuchulainn, Veldt) are marked with star symbols in the south-central portion, indicating known gold mineralisation.

## Research Value
This tenure map establishes the **current exploration and mining licence framework** immediately surrounding Coe, allowing a researcher to understand which ground is held, by whom (by cross-referencing tenement numbers with DMIRS/WAMEX records), and how it relates to known historical gold deposits (Great Cuchulainn, South Cuchulainn, Veldt). The spatial clustering of prospecting licences along a north–south structural corridor west of Coe strongly implies that the reporting company is systematically targeting a **greenstone-hosted gold system**, likely associated with the Coe Domain of the Murchison Province. Combined with other report figures (geochemistry, drilling results, geophysics), this map anchors all exploration data to a specific, legally-defined ground position and highlights the proximity of current work to historically significant, high-grade gold producers.
